concept: Inspect / Systematically Examine Condition Against Criteria
description: To examine a component, system, material, or completed work systematically in order to identify its condition and compare observations with stated criteria. The event-noun rule supplies inspection. Inspection does not itself repair, test every hidden property, certify compliance, approve use, guarantee safety, or grant the inspector authority beyond the stated task.
sound_symbolism: The complete verb 'nila' (see) opens the word, and precise 'ki' narrows that observation to an ordered, criterion-guided examination. The movement from seeing to a clear boundary keeps inspection systematic without implying approval or repair.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha terura nilaki.' (I inspect this system.) Name the criteria, method, scope, findings, and source authority separately when they matter. The event-noun rule supplies inspection. Distinct from 'nila' (see), 'morae' (sense), 'masue' (measure), 'masuro' (calibrate), and approval for service.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Inspection practices deliberate attention while keeping observation separate from certainty, judgment, and permission.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Careful examination before and after work joins embodied observation to explicit standards of craft and upkeep.

tags.activity: systematic examination of condition against criteria
tags.cognition: a structured relation among observations, scope, and assessment
tags.creation: a practice supporting maintenance, repair, and checked workmanship
